prompt: Every time our ETL job runs, it logs 50,000 lines of row-level progress. I need the job summary and any errors, not the play-by-play. Build me a filter that keeps signal and drops the noise.
My IDE extension is flooding the output panel with verbose trace logs from three different language servers. Help me configure it so only actionable diagnostics get through.
We have a shared Slack channel where bot integrations post hundreds of automated messages per day. Mute the bots but keep human messages visible, and document how to reverse it.

Our Docker Compose setup for local dev outputs interleaved logs from 12 services. Create a logging config that silences the healthy services and only shows output from the ones I'm actively debugging.

The security scanner runs nightly and generates a 200-page PDF of findings, 95% of which are accepted risks we've already triaged. Set up a suppression list for the known-acceptable findings so the new ones stand out.
I'm doing deep work on a complex bug. Temporarily suppress all GitHub notification emails, PR review requests, and Jira assignment notifications. Give me a restore plan for when I surface.

Our network monitoring tool sends identical 'link flap' alerts for the same switch port 30 times per hour. Deduplicate and suppress the repeats while keeping the first occurrence and any resolution notification.
There's too much noise in our git log — merge commits, auto-formatting commits, dependency bumps. Filter the history down to just meaningful feature and bugfix commits.
